etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "shy" + "-ness" from Old English "scēoh" + "-ness" (state of being easily frightened)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: State easily frightened; experiencing state timid and shyness state with timid development
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: State frightened → state timid → contemporary shyness state and shyness reserved with state presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: State frightened → state timid → shyness state → reserved understanding
